seed_problem: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
mutation: input+output: relax the strict bisection to a headcount BAND (| |A|-|B| | <= L) and plant a heavy friction chain so the contiguous-split baseline is deliberately weak; the balance-band max-cut on this planted, heavy-tailed instance is NP-hard and single-move / swap / multi-restart heuristics diverge.
title: Aurora Station: Two-Habitat Crew Split
strategies: contiguous-split baseline: crew 1..floor(n/2) to A, rest to B (the calibration point)
single-move local search: from the contiguous split, greedily flip each crew to the other habitat when it increases cut and keeps the headcount band feasible
swap-based local search: exchange a crew in A with a crew in B (balance-preserving) whenever it raises the cut, iterated to convergence
randomised multi-restart: many random balanced starts, local search each to a local optimum, keep the highest-cut split
